rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE M. HASBUN ET AL VS. 101 LOMBARD STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File A Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 24, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT is continued to November 2, 2022 to be heard with other pending motion and after Defendant's motion to continue trial is heard. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KEITH GOODMAN VS. AUDREY DER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant And Crosscomplainant Audrey Der???S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; And For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 24, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT AUDREY DER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS is GRANTED. Within 15 days of notice of entry of order Plaintiff to provide complete verified responses without objections to Special Interrogatories Nos. 9, 10, 21, and 35 and to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 8, 17, 24, and 26. Sanctions granted as requested to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC. VS. KIMBERLY COLEMON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 24, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT KIMBERLY COLEMON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Complaint alleges in paragraph 21 that no unlawful detainer assistant was used. To the extent, Defendant attempted to file a motion to quash re: improperly filled out summons, the motion is moot. Improperly filled out summons were issued due to a clerical error and are rescinded. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
